data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f1c476c3">
    <title>tests: remove full-tests from pre-push; rely on CI for full-suite gate</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  lefthook.yml pre-push hook runs `go test -race -cover ./...` (full
  suite, ~168s) on every push. This blocks the operator's session-end
  push for the duration of the full suite, even though CI on origin
  already runs the same suite on every push. The pre-push run is
  duplicate work and duplicate wait time. Full-suite execution is still
  required on a semi-regular basis (regression detection), but
  blocking the operator's single push at session end is the wrong
  place to do it.

  Note (per the 2026-05-09 plan-review): after sister bead
  ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the drain itself
  stops pushing entirely (operator pushes once at session end). So
  this bead's impact lands on the operator's session-end push, not on
  per-bead drain throughput.

ROOT CAUSE
  - lefthook.yml line ~572 (full-tests run command):
        `go test -race -cover ./...`
    runs on every pre-push event.
  - .github/workflows/ci.yml runs the same suite on push to origin,
    making the pre-push run redundant for regression detection.
  - There is no opt-in local target for "verify before push" workflows;
    today's only option is the always-on pre-push hook.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Edit lefthook.yml (line ~572 area): remove the `full-tests` step
     from the pre-push hook entirely. Keep all other pre-push steps
     (git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug, build, lint, etc.).
     If lefthook supports skip-by-default-but-runnable commands, mark
     full-tests skip-by-default; otherwise just delete the step from
     pre-push and surface it elsewhere.

  2. Add (or confirm) a local opt-in path for "verify before push"
     workflows:
        a. Existing Makefile `test` target runs `go test -v ./...` —
           document this as the local pre-push verification path.
        b. Optionally add a new `test-full` target that mirrors CI's
           invocation exactly (`go test -race -cover ./...`).

  3. Verify .github/workflows/ci.yml still runs the full suite without
     -short on every push to origin. (The earlier audit confirmed it
     does; just verify nothing has changed.)

  4. Update CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/) with a one-paragraph
     note on the pre-push / CI split:
        - Pre-push runs cheap safety checks (build, lint, secrets,
          git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug) for fast operator
          feedback.
        - CI on origin runs the full suite for every push.
        - Operators wanting "verify before push" run `make test` or
          `make test-full` manually.

NON-SCOPE
  - Adding -short to pre-push (deliberately superseded; full removal is
    cleaner).
  - testing.Short() skips on individual hot tests (sister bead
    ddx-1fda33d2 still useful — those skips help local `make test`
    and CI -short paths).
  - perf-tag separation (sister bead ddx-150758e7 still useful —
    perf tests should not run in default CI either).
  - Adding a local watch-and-test daemon (out of scope; operators can
    invoke make test manually).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf: drain stops pushing entirely; this bead's
    impact lands only on operator's session-end push.
  - Sister bead ddx-1fda33d2: testing.Short() skips remain valuable for
    local `make test` and CI -short paths.
  - Sister bead ddx-150758e7: perf-tag separation remains valuable.
  - Sister bead ddx-626f3120: CI test -json artifact remains valuable
    for trend visibility on the CI runs.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. lefthook.yml pre-push hook no longer contains the full-tests step that runs `go test -race -cover ./...` (or equivalent full-suite test invocation).
2. lefthook.yml pre-push hook still runs all other safety checks (build, lint, secrets, git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug).
3. .github/workflows/ci.yml continues to run the full suite (without -short) on push to origin; verify and document this is unchanged.
4. Makefile contains a `test-full` target (or the existing `test` target documents the equivalent) that runs the full suite as a local opt-in for operators wanting "verify before push" workflow.
5. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/) contains a one-paragraph note documenting the pre-push / CI / local-opt-in split.
6. lefthook run pre-push completes in noticeably less wall-clock time after this bead lands (manually verified; not asserted in test).
7. cd cli && go test ./... green (CI path; no -short).
8. cd cli && go test -short ./... green (still useful for sister beads).
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:tests, area:ci, kind:refactor, perf</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="529153308dc01dfc42023ba65eddcc5eed8f595e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260509T220325-4c05113c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed. All ACs satisfied:

1. **AC1**: `full-tests` step removed from pre-push hook in `lefthook.yml`.
2. **AC2**: All other pre-push checks (git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug) remain; all passed in pre-commit run.
3. **AC3**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` line 100 runs `go test ./... -count=1` on every push to origin — unchanged.
4. **AC4**: `make test-full` target added to `cli/Makefile`, runs `go test -race -cover ./...` with CGO_ENABLED=1 and git env scrubbing.
5. **AC5**: CLAUDE.md updated with "Pre-push / CI / local-opt-in split" section documenting the division.
6. **AC6**: Pre-push no longer runs the ~168s suite (manually verifiable by timing a push).
7. **AC7+8**: `go test ./...` and `go test -short ./...` both exit 0.
8. **AC9**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passed cleanly.
